magnitude_note: Broad Asian-crisis recovery package; RM7bn fiscal stimulus (July 1998) + RM2bn socio-economic spending; reversed the earlier 1997-98 austerity (2% across-the-board spending cut).
RM3 billion pre-emptive fiscal stimulus package announced 27 March 2001.
RM4.3 billion additional stimulus package (pakej rangsangan tambahan) announced 25 September 2001.

Retail fuel prices raised ~40.4% in June 2008 as the global crude-oil spike made the subsidy fiscally unsustainable; partial offsets (rice subsidy, fishermen/transport fuel subsidy, motorist fuel cash rebate).
First Economic Stimulus Package of RM7 billion announced November 2008 (GFC response).

act_label: Second Economic Stimulus Package, RM60bn (March 2009 mini-budget)
magnitude_note: Second Economic Stimulus Package (Pakej Rangsangan Ekonomi Kedua) of RM60 billion, March 2009 mini-budget; RM67bn GFC total with the first package.

Structural multi-year subsidy reform launched under the 10th Malaysia Plan / NEM (2010): phased cuts to fuel & sugar subsidies (sugar subsidy abolished 2013-14), evolving into targeted retargeting (PADU) by 2023. Subsidies were ~4.7% of GDP / ~RM12,900 per household p.a. at launch.

New permanent household cash-transfer scheme launched 2012 as Bantuan Rakyat 1Malaysia (BR1M ~RM500/household), expanded and rebranded BSH -> BPR -> Bantuan Keluarga Malaysia (BKM, up to ~RM2,500/household by 2022).
Prihatin Rakyat Economic Stimulus Package, RM250 billion (announced 27 March 2020; incl. PRIHATIN SME+ RM10bn, 6 April 2020). COVID-19 response.
PENJANA (Pelan Jana Semula Ekonomi Negara), RM35 billion, announced 5 June 2020. COVID-19 recovery phase.
Perlindungan Ekonomi dan Rakyat Malaysia (PERMAI), RM15 billion, announced 18 January 2021.
Program Strategik Memperkasa Rakyat dan Ekonomi (PEMERKASA), RM20 billion, announced 17 March 2021.
PEMERKASA+ , RM40 billion, announced 31 May 2021 (MCO 3.0 response).
Pakej Perlindungan Rakyat dan Pemulihan Ekonomi (PEMULIH), RM150 billion, announced 28 June 2021. Largest 2021 COVID package.
